tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Ala is the Igbo earth goddess and guardian of morality.
  • She governs fertility, land, and justice.
  • She is deeply respected in Igbo culture.
  • She ensures harmony between humans and nature.
  • She punishes those who break moral laws.

tab2.title: Powers
tab2.content:
• Earth Control – Governs land and fertility.
  • Judgment – Enforces moral laws.
  • Life Creation – Supports agriculture.
  • Spiritual Authority – Influences ancestors.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content:
• Ala is central to Igbo traditions.
  • She is both nurturing and strict.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Amadioha is the Igbo god of thunder and justice.
  • He punishes wrongdoers with lightning.
  • He represents fairness and divine law.
  • He is feared and respected.
  • He protects the innocent.

tab2.title: Powers
tab2.content:
• Lightning Control – Strikes enemies.
  • Justice – Enforces divine law.
  • Protection – Guards the innocent.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Anansi is a famous spider trickster.
  • He is known for cleverness and storytelling.
  • He often outsmarts stronger beings.
  • He represents wisdom and humor.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Asase Yaa is the earth goddess of the Akan people.
  • She represents fertility and agriculture.
  • She is the mother of all life.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: • She is honored weekly in Akan culture.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Babalu Aye is associated with disease and healing.
  • He controls epidemics.
  • He is both feared and respected.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Chukwu is the supreme god of Igbo people.
  • He is the creator of all things.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content: • Dan is a serpent deity representing balance.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content: • Erinle is a hunter and healer god.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content: • Eshu is a messenger and trickster.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content: • Heviosso is the god of thunder.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content: • Ikenga represents personal achievement.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content: • Katonda is the creator god.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Legba is a powerful gatekeeper deity in West African mythology.
  • He controls access between the human world and the spirit world.
  • He must be invoked before communicating with other gods.
  • He is both wise and mischievous.
  • He represents communication, crossroads, and destiny.

tab2.title: Powers
tab2.content:
• Gatekeeping – Controls access between worlds.
  • Communication – Acts as messenger between humans and gods.
  • Trickery – Uses intelligence and deception.
  • Spiritual Authority – Opens or blocks spiritual paths.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content:
• Legba is often associated with crossroads.
  • No ritual begins without honoring him.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Lisa is the male counterpart of Mawu in Fon mythology.
  • He represents the sun, strength, and active energy.
  • Together, Mawu and Lisa form a balanced cosmic duality.
  • Lisa symbolizes heat, power, and authority.
  • He plays a key role in maintaining universal balance.

tab2.title: Powers
tab2.content:
• Solar Energy – Controls sunlight and heat.
  • Strength – Represents power and vitality.
  • Balance – Works with Mawu to maintain harmony.
  • Authority – Symbol of divine leadership.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content:
• Lisa is often paired with Mawu as a dual deity.
  • He represents the masculine force in creation.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Mawu is a supreme creator goddess in the Fon tradition of West Africa.
  • She is often paired with Lisa, representing a duality of feminine and masculine cosmic forces.
  • Mawu is associated with the moon, coolness, wisdom, and nurturing energy.
  • She is believed to have shaped the world and given life to humanity.
  • Her presence symbolizes harmony, balance, and divine motherhood.

tab2.title: Powers
tab2.content:
• Creation – Responsible for shaping life and the natural world.
  • Balance – Maintains harmony between opposing forces.
  • Moon Influence – Governs calmness, cycles, and fertility.
  • Wisdom – Represents intelligence and guidance.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content:
• Mawu is often depicted as calm and nurturing.
  • She is central to Fon cosmology.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Mulungu is a high god worshipped across various Bantu-speaking regions.
  • He is associated with the sky, creation, and divine authority.
  • Mulungu is often seen as distant but powerful.
  • He governs the forces of nature and human destiny.
  • He represents supreme power and spiritual oversight.

tab2.title: Powers
tab2.content:
• Creation – Creator of the universe and life.
  • Authority – Supreme divine ruler.
  • Nature Control – Governs natural elements.
  • Spiritual Influence – Guides human fate.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content:
• Mulungu appears in multiple African cultures.
  • He is often considered distant from human affairs.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Nomkhubulwane is a Zulu goddess associated with fertility and rain.
  • She plays an important role in agriculture and harvest.
  • She ensures the growth of crops and survival of communities.
  • She represents renewal and life cycles.
  • She is deeply respected in traditional Zulu beliefs.

tab2.title: Powers
tab2.content:
• Rain Control – Brings rainfall for crops.
  • Fertility – Supports life and growth.
  • Nature Harmony – Maintains environmental balance.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Nyame is the supreme sky god of the Akan people.
  • He is the source of all life and authority.
  • He governs the heavens and spiritual world.
  • He is distant but omnipotent.
  • He symbolizes divine power and leadership.

tab2.title: Powers
tab2.content:
• Creation – Creator of all existence.
  • Authority – Supreme ruler.
  • Sky Control – Governs heavens.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: • Nyame is often referenced in Akan proverbs.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Nyankopon is another aspect of the supreme Akan deity.
  • He represents wisdom and divine intelligence.
  • He is closely linked with Nyame.
  • He embodies spiritual guidance.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Obatala is a major Yoruba deity associated with creation and purity.
  • He is believed to have shaped human bodies.
  • He represents wisdom and peace.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Ogun is the god of iron, war, and labor.
  • He is associated with tools and weapons.
  • He represents strength and determination.

tab2.title: Powers
tab2.content:
• Iron Control – Master of metal.
  • Strength – Powerful warrior.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content:
• Olokun rules the deep ocean.
  • He is associated with wealth and mystery.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content: • Orunmila is the deity of wisdom and divination.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content: • Oshun is the goddess of love and rivers.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content: • Oya governs storms and transformation.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content: • Ruhanga is a creator deity.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content: • Sakpata controls earth and disease.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content: • Shango is a powerful thunder god.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content: • Tano is a river god.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content: • Unkulunkulu is the first human and creator.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content: • Waaq is a sky god.

tab1.title: Overview
tab1.content: • Yemoja is the mother of all waters.
